problem_summary: Replacing the ceramic cartridge in a single-lever kitchen faucet to stop a slow drip at the spout.
safety_pre_check: Shut off the hot AND cold supply valves under the sink.
Open the tap to relieve any residual line pressure.
Place a small towel in the sink basin to catch dropped screws.
Confirm the supply is fully off before disassembling the cartridge.

parts_summary.name: Replacement ceramic cartridge
parts_summary.specification: Match the existing cartridge brand + size (typically 35 mm or 40 mm)

parts_summary.name: Plumber's grease
parts_summary.specification: Silicone-based grease for the new cartridge O-rings

tools_summary.name: Allen key set
tools_summary.specification: Usually 2.5 mm hex for the handle grub screw

tools_summary.name: Adjustable wrench
tools_summary.specification: To loosen the cartridge retaining nut

tools_summary.name: Soft cloth
tools_summary.specification: Protects the chrome from wrench marks

scene_lock.subject: A modern single-lever brushed-chrome kitchen mixer tap mounted at the back of a stainless-steel sink.
scene_lock.environment: Bright kitchen counter, white tile splashback, daylight from a side window, neutral indoor lighting.
scene_lock.hands_style: Adult hands wearing yellow nitrile household gloves.
scene_lock.style: Photorealistic instructional video style with clean macro detail and natural daylight.
scene_lock.color_palette: Chrome silver tap, stainless steel sink, white tile, yellow gloves, neutral daylight.
consistency_phrases: Keep the same chrome single-lever tap across all shots
Show only the spout drip until the cartridge is replaced

steps.title: Shut off the water supply
steps.description: Open the cabinet under the sink, close both the hot and cold supply valves clockwise, then open the kitchen tap to release any remaining pressure.
steps.subject_focus: The two angle-stop supply valves under the sink.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: Inside the cabinet under the kitchen sink. Subject: Yellow-gloved hands reach for the hot (red) supply valve. Details: Photorealistic, copper supply lines clearly visible, indirect daylight.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: Same cabinet. Subject: Both supply valves are now in the closed position (handles perpendicular to the line). Details: Photorealistic, visibly closed valves.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the hand turning the hot supply valve clockwise to closed, then the cold one, then briefly opening the kitchen tap above to confirm no flow.
steps.narration: Open the cabinet under the sink and close both the hot and cold supply valves, then open the tap to release any remaining pressure.
steps.subtitle: Shut off the water.
steps.safety_note: Confirm the water is fully off before opening the cartridge — a pressurized line will spray.
steps.success_criteria: Both supply valves fully closed and the tap runs dry.
steps.common_mistake: Only closing one of the two supply lines.

steps.title: Remove the handle and retaining nut
steps.description: Pop off the decorative cap on the handle, loosen the grub screw with the Allen key, lift the lever off, then unscrew the chrome retaining nut to expose the cartridge.
steps.subject_focus: The handle, grub screw, and chrome retaining nut.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: Kitchen counter. Subject: Yellow-gloved hands hold a small Allen key against the side of the faucet handle where the grub screw sits. Details: Photorealistic close-up, brushed chrome reflecting daylight.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: Same kitchen. Subject: The lever handle has been removed and set aside, and the chrome retaining nut has been unscrewed and lifted, exposing the brass-bodied ceramic cartridge in the faucet base. Details: Photorealistic macro, internal cartridge clearly visible.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the Allen key loosening the grub screw, the handle sliding off, then the adjustable wrench (cushioned with cloth) loosening the retaining nut.
steps.narration: Pop the cap, loosen the grub screw with the Allen key, lift the handle off, and unscrew the chrome retaining nut.
steps.subtitle: Remove the handle and nut.
steps.safety_note: Cushion the chrome with a soft cloth to avoid scratching it.
steps.success_criteria: Handle removed and the cartridge body is now accessible.
steps.common_mistake: Forgetting to release line pressure first — water sprays out when the nut comes off.

steps.title: Swap the cartridge and reassemble
steps.description: Pull the old cartridge straight up, drop the new one in matching the alignment tabs, grease the O-rings, then reverse the disassembly to put the faucet back together.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: Kitchen counter. Subject: Yellow-gloved hands pull the old ceramic cartridge straight up out of the brass faucet body. Details: Photorealistic macro, alignment tabs visible.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: Same kitchen. Subject: The new cartridge is seated correctly with alignment tabs in place, the retaining nut hand-tightened then snugged with the wrench, the lever back on, and the supply valves reopened — water flows from the tap cleanly with no drip. Details: Photorealistic, no water leaking, clean reassembled faucet.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the old cartridge lifted out, the new one greased and dropped in with alignment, the nut and handle reassembled, and finally the supply valves reopened with water flowing cleanly.
steps.narration: Lift the old cartridge out, drop the new one in matching the alignment tabs, then reverse the steps to reassemble and turn the water back on.
steps.subtitle: Install the new cartridge.
steps.safety_note: Do not over-tighten the retaining nut — hand-tight plus a quarter turn is enough.
steps.success_criteria: Tap operates smoothly with no drip at the spout.
steps.common_mistake: Installing the new cartridge rotated 180° — the hot/cold sides will be swapped.
